faults.description: No alarm trigger signal exists in the Taxi-User API (Section 3.2.1) for the driver to activate the alarm. No alarm notification signal exists in the Taxi-Central API (Section 3.4.2) to notify the central when an alarm is triggered. The design includes Reset_Alarm and AlarmReset for resetting, but provides no mechanism to initiate an alarm. The central can therefore never be notified when a driver activates the alarm.
faults.requirement_reference: Req 3.1.17 (central shall be notified immediately when alarm is turned on), Req 3.1.18 (voice channel shall be established immediately when alarm is turned on)

faults.use_case: UC 1.7 – Central: Handle incoming alarms / UC 1.8 – Taxi: Alarm event
faults.description: Reset_Alarm(integer) is listed as a signal FROM the user (taxi driver) TO the taxi module in Section 3.2.1. This means the design allows the driver to reset the alarm, which directly violates Req 3.1.21. According to UC 1.7 Task 3, only the operator is responsible for resetting the alarm. The signal should only exist in the Central-Operator API (Section 3.3.1, where AlarmReset is correctly placed).
faults.requirement_reference: Req 3.1.21 (it shall not be possible for the driver to reset the alarm)

faults.description: The Position(integer, integer) signal in Section 3.4.2 carries only taxi number and zone number. UC 1.8 Task 3 requires the taxi to send exact coordinates to the central every 30 seconds during an alarm. Req 3.1.19 explicitly states 'exact position' must be sent. The design provides no signal capable of transmitting GPS coordinates; Position only transmits a zone identifier.
faults.requirement_reference: Req 3.1.19 (taxi shall send exact position to central every 30 seconds while alarm is on)

faults.description: The LoginSignal in Section 3.2.1 has no parameters. UC 1.4 Task 2 states the driver swipes an identification card, which should carry driver identity. Req 3.1.3 requires the login to be confirmed by taxi number and driver number. Without parameters, the taxi module receives no driver identification from the terminal, making driver-specific login impossible.
faults.requirement_reference: Req 3.1.3 (log in shall be confirmed by taxi number and driver number)

faults.description: The LoggedIn(integer) signal in Section 3.4.2 carries only the taxi number. UC 1.4 Task 3 explicitly requires the terminal to send both position AND driver information to the central. Req 3.1.6 states that position information shall be sent to the central when logging in. The signal is missing driver number and position data. Table 1 confirms LoggedIn (type 3) has no data fields at all.
faults.requirement_reference: Req 3.1.6 (position shall be sent to central when driver is logging in), Req 3.1.3 (login confirmed by taxi number and driver number)

faults.description: Section 3.4.1 (Central to Taxi signals) contains LoginOk(integer) but no LoginFailed signal. UC 1.4 Variant 4b states that if the card is not valid, the central rejects the driver. Table 1 has no LoginFailed message type. Without this signal, the central cannot inform the taxi module of a failed login, so the Taxi-User API signal LoginFailed(integer) in Section 3.2.2 can never be triggered via the central validation path.
faults.requirement_reference: Req 3.1.5 (system shall notify driver whether log in was successfully done or not)

faults.description: The Order_struct in Section 3.1 defines fields: Order_number, Time, Dispatch_time, Address, Name, Misc. The Priority field is missing. UC 1.2 Task 2 explicitly states orders can be prioritized ('some customers are more important than others'). The database ER-diagram in the requirements (Figure 2) lists Priority as an Order field. Without this field in Order_struct, order priority cannot be transmitted through the system.
faults.requirement_reference: UC 1.2 Task 2 (order can be prioritized), Requirements Figure 2 (Order entity has Priority field)

faults.description: The Order_struct in Section 3.1 is missing a Taxi field to designate which specific taxi should handle the order. Req 3.2.21 presupposes this field: 'If the order has the property taxi set to zero the system will decide to which car the order is dispatched.' The database design (Figure 2 in requirements) shows Taxi_number as an Order field. Without it, directed dispatch to a specific taxi is not supported by the data structure.
faults.requirement_reference: Req 3.2.21 (if order property taxi is zero, system decides; implies taxi property must exist)

faults.description: MSC 4.1 shows Not_Avail(Taxi_Nbr) sent from Taxi to Central only after Meter_On. However, when the driver accepts an order (Ok/Ack), the car transitions from state 'Available' to 'Waiting for customer'. This state change should also trigger a Not_Avail signal per Req 3.1.12. The MSC omits the Not_Avail for the Available→Waiting-for-customer transition, meaning the central is not informed when the taxi first becomes unavailable upon order acceptance.
faults.requirement_reference: Req 3.1.12 (when a taxi switches states, the central shall be informed of it)

faults.description: UC 1.6 Task 8 requires the driver to send arrival zone AND estimated time to the central when confident of arrival. Req 3.1.11 states 'the time and zone shall be sent to the system.' The design provides Position(integer, integer) carrying only zone (no time) and Not_Avail(integer) carrying only taxi number. No signal in Section 3.4.2 supports transmitting both zone and estimated arrival time simultaneously for the 'Soon Available' state.
faults.requirement_reference: Req 3.1.11 (when driver knows arrival time, time and zone shall be sent to the system)

faults.use_case: UC 1.12 – Central: Log in to central system
faults.description: Section 3.3.1 (Signals from the operator to the central module) contains no login or logout signals. UC 1.12 Tasks 1–3 require the operator to enter user identification and password, the system to verify it, and then log the user on. Req 3.2.3 requires this to be confirmed by user identification and password. Without a login signal in the Central-Operator API, operators cannot authenticate through the defined interface.
faults.requirement_reference: Req 3.2.3 (log in shall be confirmed by user identification and password), Req 3.2.4 (system shall notify operator if log in was successful)

faults.description: The Text_Message(integer, integer, charstring) signal in Section 3.3.1 uses its first integer parameter as receiving taxi number (if 0, all taxis are receivers). UC 1.11 Task 3 requires the destination to be 'a specific car, a group of cars, or all cars.' Req 3.2.28 states 'It shall be possible to send text messages to a group of cars.' The current signal structure only supports single car or all cars; there is no mechanism to address an arbitrary group.
faults.requirement_reference: Req 3.2.28 (it shall be possible to send text messages to a group of cars)

faults.use_case: UC 1.11 – Central: Send text message / UC 1.19 – Taxi: Receive text message
faults.description: UC 1.11 Task 4 states that each car receiving a text message shall automatically acknowledge reception to the central. Req 3.1.35 confirms this requirement. Section 3.4.2 (Taxi to Central signals) contains no text message acknowledgement signal. Table 1 also contains no corresponding message type. The existing Ack signal (Message Type 1) is for order acceptance and is not applicable here.
faults.requirement_reference: Req 3.1.35 (cars shall automatically send acknowledgement when they receive a text message)

faults.description: Section 3.2.1 contains LoginSignal for driver login but no corresponding logout signal. Req 3.1.8 states that a driver shall be able to log out when already logged in and in state Available. Req 3.1.9 requires the logout to be confirmed by taxi number and driver number. Without a logout trigger in the Taxi-User API, the driver has no defined mechanism through the terminal to initiate log out.
faults.requirement_reference: Req 3.1.8 (driver shall be able to log out if already logged in and in state available), Req 3.1.9 (log out confirmed by taxi number and driver number)

faults.description: The Taxi(integer, integer, integer) signal in Section 3.3.1 is described as: 'If the operator requests a specific taxi, this is the response.' A response signal should be in Section 3.3.2 (Signals from the central module to the operator), not in Section 3.3.1 (Signals from the operator to the central module). The signal direction is inverted; it is misplaced in the wrong API section.
faults.requirement_reference: UC 1.10 Task 2 (operator selects a car to send the order to, based on taxi number)

faults.location: Section 3.2.1 (Taxi-User API), Section 3.4.1 (Central-Taxi API), Section 3.4.2 (Taxi-Central API)
faults.description: UC 1.16 Tasks 1–2 require the driver to request car and driver properties and the central to send them to the terminal. No request signal exists in Section 3.2.1 (User→Taxi) for the driver to initiate this. No signal exists in Section 3.4.2 (Taxi→Central) to forward the request to the central. No signal exists in Section 3.4.1 (Central→Taxi) to deliver the properties to the taxi. The entire use case is unaddressed by the API design.
faults.requirement_reference: Req 3.1.49 (drivers shall be able to view their own properties)

faults.description: UC 1.18 Tasks 2–4 require the operator to select a driver and update their properties, and the system to acknowledge the update. Section 3.3.1 contains no signal for updating driver properties. Req 3.2.36 states 'It shall be possible to update driver properties' and Req 3.2.38 requires system acknowledgement of the update. Neither capability is represented in the defined API.
faults.requirement_reference: Req 3.2.36 (it shall be possible to update driver properties), Req 3.2.38 (system shall acknowledge when driver properties are updated)
